title: How to Create a WGC Campaign
author: Neela Thiagarajan

overview.business_outcome: Planners understand what a WGC Campaign is, when to use it, and how it differs from PMC before starting creation
overview.process_stage: Task Plan Creation → Standard Work Order Creation → WGC Campaign Creation
overview.data_triggers: Campaign number must match SWO number · Campaign Type WGC cannot be changed after creation · WGC does not auto-refresh · Campaign number and SWO number flow to Azure ODS with every generated work order
overview.content:
A WGC (Work Generation Campaign) in Asset Hub ties a Standard Work Order to a set of railcars and generates maintenance work orders for each car in a single one-time operation.

WGC campaigns are used for corrective or targeted maintenance — when a specific set of cars needs a defined set of work done once. They are NOT for recurring preventive maintenance (use PMC campaigns for that).

Key characteristics of a WGC Campaign:
- One-time only — work orders are generated once. Campaign does not auto-refresh.
- Requires a Standard Work Order — which carries the Task Plan, billing codes, and documents
- Campaign number must match Standard Work Order number exactly (TTX MAPMS naming convention)
- For additional cars after completion, create a new campaign with a suffix (e.g. MAPMS8262_2)

When a WGC Campaign generates work orders, Asset Hub sends the campaign number, SWO number, and campaign description to Azure ODS via Databridge Pro for downstream reporting. ODS aggregates related campaigns by SWO number.

prerequisites.business_outcome: Campaign creation succeeds on first attempt — no rework from missing Task Plan, wrong SWO reference, or naming mismatch
prerequisites.data_triggers: Task Plan must exist before SWO can link it · SWO must exist before Campaign can reference it · Campaign number should match SWO number · Car list or filter criteria must be defined
prerequisites.content:
Before creating a WGC Campaign you must have:

1. Task Plan already created and saved — refer to MAINT-TP-001
   The Task Plan defines checklist items, billing codes, and documents
   that will appear on all generated work orders

2. Standard Work Order already created with Task Plan linked on the Activities tab
   Refer to MAINT-SWO-001. The SWO must exist before the campaign can reference it.

3. Campaign number decided — should match the SWO number exactly
   TTX naming convention: MAPMS + 4-digit identifier (e.g. MAPMS8262)
   Not system-enforced but required for ODS reporting aggregation
   For split campaigns: MAPMS8262_1, MAPMS8262_2 — SWO number stays constant

4. Campaign Car List or filter criteria defined
   Either a pre-built car list (EQP-CAMPAIGN-CL-001) or filter criteria ready to enter
   Confirm car count is under 20,000 — larger lists will be blocked at retrieval

5. Asset Hub access with campaign creation permissions

steps.business_outcome: WGC Campaign created, car list generated, work orders generated for all target cars — campaign complete
steps.process_stage: WGC Campaign Creation → Validate → Activate → Verify
steps.data_triggers: Campaign Type WGC immutable after creation · Save required at every status transition · Status change alone does not trigger background jobs · Car count must be under 20,000 before Validate
steps.content:
Steps to Create a WGC Campaign:

PHASE 1 — Create Campaign Header:
1. Navigate: Work > Work Order Planning > Campaigns
2. Click the New Record icon
3. Enter the Campaign number — should match the SWO number (MAPMS convention)
   For split campaigns: use underscore suffixes (MAPMS8262_1, MAPMS8262_2)
4. Enter a Description
5. Confirm Organization = TTX
6. Set Campaign Type = WGC
   CRITICAL: Campaign Type cannot be changed after creation.
   If wrong type selected — delete the record and create a new one.
7. In the Standard Work Order field — search and select the SWO from MAINT-SWO-001
8. Click Save and OK on the confirmation message

PHASE 2 — Define Car Population:
1. Click on the Car Level Criteria tab
2. Choose one approach:
   Option A — Criteria-based: set Car Level Criteria = Yes, define filter criteria
   Option B — Direct load: leave all criteria = No, manually load car numbers to Railcar Equipment tab
3. Confirm car count is under 20,000 before proceeding
   System blocks equipment retrieval if over the limit — must split campaign
4. Click Save

PHASE 3 — Validate to Generate Car List:
1. Change Status field to Validate
2. Click Save — SAVE IS REQUIRED to trigger the equipment retrieval background job
3. Wait for status to change to Car List Generated
   Equipment retrieval runs every 5 minutes in test, daily in production
4. Click Refresh periodically to check progress
   Cars matching filter criteria appear on Railcar Equipment tab when complete

PHASE 4 — Activate to Generate Work Orders:
1. Once Status = Car List Generated, change Status to Active
2. Click Save — SAVE IS REQUIRED to trigger work order generation background job
3. Work orders generate for all cars in the campaign list
   Generation runs every 5 minutes in test, daily in production
4. Click Refresh to monitor progress

VERIFY:
- Navigate to Work > Work Orders
- Filter by Campaign ID (enter campaign number, click Run)
- Open one work order and check 8 verification points — see Verification section

verification.business_outcome: All generated work orders confirmed to contain correct data — campaign officially complete
verification.data_triggers: Campaign code and SWO code must be present on every generated work order · Scheduled Start Date = generation date not a future date · Last Meter Reading populated at generation
verification.content:
After campaign activation and work order generation, verify all 8 points before closing the campaign:

Navigate: Work > Work Orders
Filter by Campaign ID — enter the campaign number and click Run
Open one generated work order and confirm:

1. Campaign code is present
2. Standard Work Order code is present
3. Description carried over from the campaign
4. Checklist items from the Task Plan are present
5. Billing relief codes from the Task Plan are present
6. Primary document (Copy to Work Order) is present in the Documents tab
7. Scheduled Start Date = date work order was generated — NOT a future date
   WGC has no PM schedule so Scheduled Start Date = generation date
   This distinguishes WGC work orders from PMC work orders in ODS reporting
8. Last Meter Reading is populated for the car

All 8 items must be confirmed before the campaign is considered complete.

rules.business_outcome: WGC Campaign configuration errors prevented — correct work orders generated on first run
rules.data_triggers: Campaign Type immutable · Copy disabled · Campaign number drives ODS aggregation · Save required at status transitions · 20,000 car limit at equipment retrieval
rules.content:
Critical Rules — WGC Campaign:

CREATION ORDER IS MANDATORY
Task Plan must exist before Standard Work Order.
Standard Work Order must exist before WGC Campaign.
Each component must exist before the next can reference it.

CAMPAIGN TYPE WGC CANNOT BE CHANGED AFTER CREATION
Selecting WGC locks the campaign to one-time work order generation.
If wrong type is selected — delete the campaign record and create a new one.
This cannot be corrected on an existing record.

WGC DOES NOT AUTO-REFRESH
Work orders are generated once. Campaign does not repeat on a schedule.
For additional cars after campaign completes — create a new campaign with a suffix
(MAPMS8262_2) referencing the same Standard Work Order.

COPY BUTTON IS DISABLED
The Copy icon on the campaign screen is intentionally disabled.
Always create a new campaign record — do not attempt to copy an existing campaign.

CAMPAIGN NUMBER SHOULD MATCH SWO NUMBER
TTX naming convention: MAPMS + 4-digit identifier (e.g. MAPMS8262).
Not system-enforced but required for ODS to aggregate related work orders.
For split campaigns: SWO number stays constant, campaign numbers get suffixes.

SCHEDULED START DATE = GENERATION DATE
WGC has no PM schedule so Scheduled Start Date on generated work orders equals
the date the work order was created — not a planned future date.
This distinguishes WGC work orders from PMC work orders in ODS reporting.

SAVE REQUIRED AT EVERY STATUS TRANSITION
Changing status to Validate or Active without clicking Save does not trigger
the background jobs. Status change alone has no effect — Save is required.

20,000 CAR LIMIT
Car level filter criteria must return fewer than 20,000 records.
System blocks equipment retrieval if over the limit.
Split campaigns using underscore suffixes, keeping SWO number constant.
Most splits are 1 to 3 campaigns. Component-level campaigns hit this limit faster.

troubleshoot.business_outcome: WGC Campaign failures resolved — work orders generated correctly for all target cars
troubleshoot.data_triggers: Equipment retrieval on schedule · Work order generation on schedule · Task Plan must be linked at generation time · Save required at status transitions
troubleshoot.content:
WGC Campaign Troubleshooting:

Problem: Standard Work Order not visible in campaign lookup
Cause: SWO not saved, wrong organization, or recently created and not yet indexed.
Fix: Verify the SWO was saved. Search by partial number. Confirm Organization = TTX.
If recently created, refresh and try again.

Problem: Status stays at Validate — never reaches Car List Generated
Cause: Equipment retrieval job has not run yet, or filter criteria return no cars.
Fix: Equipment retrieval runs on a schedule — every 5 minutes in test, daily in production.
Click Refresh and wait. If no progress after the expected interval, confirm filter
criteria are returning cars and car count is under 20,000.

Problem: Work orders not generated after campaign set to Active
Cause: Work order generation background job has not run yet.
Fix: Wait for the job to run (up to 5 minutes in test, daily in production) and search again.
If still empty, contact the Hexagon team to confirm the generation job ran.

Problem: Task Plan data missing from generated work orders
(Checklist items, billing codes, or documents not on work orders)
Cause: Task Plan was not correctly linked on the Standard Work Order Activities tab
at the time of work order generation, or SWO was not correctly referenced on the campaign.
Fix: Verify Task Plan is linked on SWO Activities tab (trade STAR, qty 1).
Verify SWO is correctly referenced on the campaign.
If the link was missing at generation time — delete the generated work orders
and rerun work order generation after correcting the SWO.

Problem: Car list over 20,000 — retrieval blocked
Cause: Filter criteria return more than 20,000 car records.
Fix: Split into multiple campaigns using underscore suffixes (MAPMS8262_1, MAPMS8262_2).
Keep the SWO number constant across all splits.
Most campaigns split into 1 to 3 parts. Component-level campaigns may need more splits.

integration.business_outcome: Planners understand how WGC work order data flows to ODS and downstream systems for reporting
integration.data_triggers: Campaign number and SWO number sent to ODS with every generated work order · Near-real-time async via Databridge Pro · 3 retries for connectivity errors
integration.content:
WGC Campaign ODS Integration:

When a WGC Campaign generates work orders, Asset Hub sends work order and campaign
data to Azure ODS via Databridge Pro for downstream reporting and system consumption.

WHAT IS SENT:
- Campaign number
- Standard Work Order number
- Campaign description
- Work order data (full work order table fields)

HOW IT IS SENT:
- Middleware: Databridge Pro (HxGN EAM Flex Python Studio)
- Transport: SFTP flat file
- Integration cluster: EINO-019 to EINO-027 (HxGN EAM → Azure ODS)
- Timing: Near-real-time asynchronous

WHO RECEIVES IT DOWNSTREAM:
Azure ODS distributes to: Equipment Guide, Service Now, SCT, Repair Class Automation,
Maintenance Notification, Invoicing Web App, AIC, EDW, MPS

ERROR HANDLING:
- 3 automatic retries for connectivity errors
- Business/data errors: no retry — immediate email to administrators
- BOD errors visible on Databridge Message Status screen in EAM — supports manual retry
- Full traceability in Databridge Pro Dataflow Studio — Data Provenance
- Message retention: 10 days (Databridge), 30 days (EAM Message Status screen)

For field-level payload specification: see TAR-035 and TAR-040 on TTX SharePoint.

glossary.business_outcome: Users understand key WGC Campaign terms before creating or troubleshooting
glossary.data_triggers: Term definitions — reference only, no system actions triggered
glossary.content:
WGC Campaign Glossary:

WGC Campaign (Work Generation Campaign): One-time corrective maintenance campaign that generates work orders for a targeted set of railcars. References a Standard Work Order as the work instruction template.

Campaign Type WGC: The campaign type that locks the record to one-time work order generation. Cannot be changed after creation.

Standard Work Order (SWO): Work instruction template that the WGC Campaign references. SWO number must match Campaign number. Carries the Task Plan, billing codes, and documents to generated work orders.

Car Level Criteria: Tab on the WGC Campaign where filter criteria are defined to select target railcars. Options: criteria-based (dynamic filter) or direct load (manual car list). WGC does not auto-refresh regardless of setting.

Validate (Status): Status transition that triggers the equipment retrieval background job. Populates the Railcar Equipment tab with matching cars. Requires Save to trigger.

Car List Generated (Status): Status reached when equipment retrieval job completes. Car list is visible on Railcar Equipment tab and ready for activation.

Active (Status): Status transition that triggers work order generation background job. One work order created per car in the campaign list. Requires Save to trigger.

MAPMS Naming Convention: TTX convention for campaign and SWO naming — MAPMS + 4-digit identifier (e.g. MAPMS8262). Campaign number and SWO number must match. ODS aggregates related work orders by SWO number.

Split Campaign: When a car list exceeds 20,000 records, the campaign is split into multiple campaigns using underscore suffixes (MAPMS8262_1, MAPMS8262_2). SWO number stays constant across all splits.

20,000 Car Limit: Maximum number of car records a single campaign car list can contain. System blocks equipment retrieval above this limit. Conservative during cloud stabilization — may be raised.

ODS (Azure Operational Data Store): Downstream reporting system that receives work order and campaign data from Asset Hub via Databridge Pro. Aggregates related campaigns by SWO number.

Databridge Pro: The middleware component (HxGN EAM Flex Python Studio) that handles outbound data flow from Asset Hub to Azure ODS. Uses SFTP flat file transport. Supports 3 automatic retries for connectivity errors.

default_process_stage: WGC Campaign Creation → Validate → Activate → Verify
default_outcome: Work orders generated for all target railcars with correct Task Plan content, billing codes, and documents
